name: workplace_safety_satellite_derived_oakline_st_lt
premise: Two departments published incompatible guidance; frontline staff are improvising and liability is concentrating on one signature line. In Workplace Safety, the situation tagged internally around workplace safety satellite derived oakline st lt has converged with local pressure in the Oakline corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Finance notes that the fastest mitigation spends down a restricted reserve that another program legally depends on next quarter. An internal red-team note and a customer ticket describe the same failure mode with incompatible timelines. Regulators or auditors are en route with a ninety-minute ETA; the first documented response will anchor every later question. The trade is not abstract: it is short term versus long term under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Keisha Chandra
agents.identity: I am Keisha Chandra, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Workplace Safety. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat long term as disposable collateral every time short term spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Elena Navarro
agents.identity: I am Elena Navarro, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Workplace Safety. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so short term cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes long term in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if short term advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for long term.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Keisha Chandra must choose under short term versus long term. What does Keisha Chandra do?
